mission: Own AquaSynth patch authoring, analysis, preset mapping, and Faust generation in C# for the AquaSynth/Vortice engine path.

own_here: ZynAddSubFX .xiz feature extraction with project-authored fixtures
Zyn PAD spectral-cloud DSL surface with explicit Zyn profile/bandwidth/position table semantics
staged rate/level envelopes for normal voices
DX7 measured carrier output amplitude curve
DX7 ROM COM output compensation cut
DX7 operator key/rate scaling at effective played note
DX7 block-interpolated envelope applied-gain trace helper

taste: Patch scripts should be terse but lower into explicit, inspectable graph data.
Compiler abstractions should remove repeated musical structure, not hide DSP behavior.
Generated Faust should be boring enough to diff and debug.
Realtime-facing work must assume allocation, locks, file I/O, console I/O, and OS calls are hostile.

reference_strategy.purpose: Use external synths and DSP corpora as parity pressure for AquaSynth's patch DSL and Faust emitter: the DSL must reproduce behavior in terse, readable AquaSynth syntax, not clone foreign internals.
priority_order: DX7/Dexed SysEx for FM operator topology
ZynAddSubFX instruments for additive, subtractive, PAD, formant, and layered patches
Faust examples/libraries for general DSP graph topology
Surge XT for modern hybrid synth modulation
Cardinal/VCV-style patches for modular cable/rate semantics
reference_strategy.rule: Add DSL abstractions only when reference rebuilds prove the missing invariant. Do not consume references that cannot be backed by parity tests; parsing alone is inventory, not evidence of expressivity.

dx7_reference_path.status: DX7 SysEx parser extracts voice edit buffers, packed voices, packed 32-voice banks, six operators, envelopes, algorithm, feedback, oscillator sync, LFO, transpose, voice name, checksum validation, ReferencePatch features, algorithm topology from the reverse-engineered OPS algorithm ROM table, envelope-to-ADSR approximations, measured carrier output amplitude, isolated two-op modulation route scale, summed/cascaded route scale, calibrated feedback amount table, ratio detune lowering, voice transpose lowering, fixed-frequency operator lowering, ROM COM output compensation cut, operator key/rate scaling at the effective played note, topology-aware route lowering, staged operator envelopes with per-segment curves, raw and block-interpolated DX7 envelope tracing, applied-envelope curved lowering with optional sustain floor, two AquaSynth DSL rebuild attempts, first-class OperatorGraph support with graph-level vibrato, public-domain SysEx fixture coverage, dexed-py reference rendering, project-authored Dexed patch rendering, AquaSynth candidate rendering through Faust-generated C#, one thresholded rendered parity test for analog1.syx voice 13, a hard PRC SYNTH1 parity probe with listening WAV artifacts and log-mel <= 0.145, project-authored algorithm-8 summed-stack/cascade/summed-pair parity, rendered community parity for Mooger, Piano Bass, RES SYNTH1, and ANLGSYN 1 with a sustained high-band gate, a MELLOWSOLO pressure artifact test, and renderable delayed operator feedback.
dx7_reference_path.lesson: Algorithm 32's additive six-carrier shape maps cleanly to six AquaSynth voices. Algorithm 8 justified OperatorGraph: operators, modulation edges, carriers, feedback, and graph-level pitch motion belong to a graph authority, not Voice fields. The first rendered parity rung is behavioral and sine-like, not exact DX7 operator execution. A harder algorithm-8 target exposed that feedback must be delayed recursion in Faust, not a direct cyclic self-reference. Static DX7 output-level approximation helped import/rebuild math but was mathematically wrong for carrier amplitude. A single-carrier Dexed sweep shows carrier amplitude follows 2^((outputLevel - 99) / 8). Isolated two-op DX7 FM maps full-level modulation to AquaSynth route index 6.275; summed/cascaded algorithm-8 branches now use 6.0 after PRC listening, a project-authored combined stack probe, and split cascade/summed-pair probes showed lower values were under-bright. Feedback lowers through a fitted table instead of PRC-local constants; ANLGSYN 1 showed max feedback value 7 needs a hotter AquaSynth amount 2.2 to recover buzzy high bands, while value 5 remains 0.19 for PRC. ANLGSYN 1 then exposed a sustained-buzz gap: output soft clip helped but did not stop mid-note drift; max-feedback source operators need an applied-envelope sustain floor so the feedback loop stays driven after the attack. ANLGSYN 1's remaining beat is pitch LFO: AMD is nonzero but all operator AMS values are 0, while PMD/PMS lower cleanly to operator-graph vibrato with faded delay. Ratio-mode detune belongs in operator frequency lowering. DX7 voice transpose belongs in graph base frequency: transpose 24 is neutral, transpose 12 lowers MIDI note 60 to 130.8128 Hz. DX7 fixed-frequency operators are absolute Hz, not note ratios; ANLGSYN 1 proved fixed op1/op3 must lower to ratios around 0.007615/0.009227 against a 130.8128 Hz graph base to preserve low buzzing modulation. ROM COM carrier compensation was cut: user listening found no-COM renders harmonically closer and mainly too quiet, so `OperatorOutputCompensation` returns unity and graph gain owns loudness recovery. Operator key-scaling and rate-scaling must lower against the effective played MIDI note after transpose; hardcoded MIDI 60 made RES SYNTH1 too soft on attack and Piano Bass less harmonically accurate. Project-authored Dexed patch rendering now gives lawful calibration targets. Reusing the operator output curve for EG levels was tested and cut because it failed hard-target log-mel; later output-level-dependent and naive traced-release applied-envelope experiments were also cut because they worsened broader parity. Direct envelope tracing shows current linear `env=rl` cannot express DX7 EG spike/drop behavior. Dexed-style audio samples EG once per 64-sample block and interpolates applied gain, so raw EG parity alone is not audio parity. Staged operator envelopes now support linear/exponential segments and supranormal levels. `ApproximateAppliedRateLevelEnvelope` uses that surface and now picks the first near-peak before gate so slower rising modulators hit early instead of swelling late; it can also preserve a floor for max-feedback source sustain. Community gains with no COM and played-note scaling: Mooger 0.75, Piano Bass 0.72, RES SYNTH1 0.75. Algorithm-2 non-carrier applied envelopes currently cap the first peak at 0.92 to prevent ANLGSYN 1/RES SYNTH1 peak blowout while preserving Piano Bass. MELLOWSOLO is a pressure artifact test, not passing parity; its render window is 1.25s so the candidate release is audible instead of cut at the artifact boundary. Mooger needs a more aggressive attack contour, and RES SYNTH1's phase-like attack issue is likely relative operator attack/peak shape because oscillator sync is enabled. PRC remains passing on log-mel/envelope/RMS, with score floor lowered to 0.74 after rate scaling changed the aggregate score.
dx7_reference_path.next: Use the now-validated algorithm-8 route scale, transpose lowering, and fixed-frequency lowering as fixed inputs while isolating remaining community-voice timbre mismatch, then expand to broader public-domain banks.

zyn_reference_path.status: Initial ZynInstrumentReader parses plain XML and gzip-compressed .xiz-shaped XML from project-authored fixtures into ZynInstrument records and ReferencePatch features. First Zyn-style AquaSynth rebuilds exist for additive lead, PAD texture, and vocal/formant layer. `ZynInstrumentSurvey` can rank .xiz directories by feature pressure. Named `layer` declarations provide the first Zyn-driven DSL abstraction; additive `harmonics`, PAD `spectrum`, and staged `env=rl` voice envelopes now attach to those layers. Zyn PAD `spectrum` banks can now carry explicit `zyn_mode`, `zyn_bandwidth`, `zyn_bwscale`, `zyn_profile`, and `zyn_position` table-generation fields, and `PadSynthWaveform` lowers those through Zyn-style profile/bandwidth math. The pinned Zyn source now builds as a test-only MSYS2/MinGW oracle through `tools/zyn-reference/build-zyn-reference.ps1`, which patches an ignored artifact source copy, proves `PadNoteTest`, and builds `ZynPadReference`.
zyn_reference_path.lesson: Start Zyn as structural classification, then move into explicit scoped rebuilds. Upstream Zyn presets live in a nested GPL instrument-bank submodule and may be used as test-only parity/pressure feedstock once provenance is recorded. The ZynAddSubFX engine source is pinned as GPL test/development material under `external/zynaddsubfx` at 3ab608c432996ba4d582176572c0b0f82328c825; use it as a parity oracle only, never as AquaSynth runtime or NuGet package content. Zyn build fixes belong in `tools/zyn-reference/patches/` and apply to ignored artifact copies, not the clean pinned submodule. The PAD oracle now renders both generated PAD sample tables and note audio through Zyn `PADnoteParameters`/`PADnote`; optional parity artifacts live under `artifacts/parity/zyn-pad-reference` and `artifacts/parity/zyn-pad-fixtures`. The first project-authored PAD comparison proved the harness and showed AquaSynth `spectrum` was not a close Zyn PAD emulation. User listening identified Zyn as pure wineglass and AquaSynth as angry bees. Cutting unearned air/noise/drive/soft-clip from the rebuild, adding interpolated wavetable reads, and raising AquaSynth spectral tables to Zyn's 131072-frame PAD size improved the report. A later pitch/drone pass split PAD table root from playback note frequency: `spectrum root=...` owns table/base frequency and `freq=...` owns the played note. Using Zyn's reported sample basefreq 77.7813 while playing 261.6256, removing gratuitous spread/vibrato, collapsing two same-note PAD voices into one coherent spectral table, and trimming gain improved the report to log-mel 0.35669, envelope 0.347572, RMS ratio 1.001704, centroid ratio 1.004072, score 0.6211. The first PAD fixture survey shows `pad-pure` and the old `pad-texture` render identically through Zyn, so the filter/category XML in `pad-texture` is not live pressure for this oracle path. `pad-harmonic` changes the source table and note output through explicit OSCIL harmonics, making it the next real PAD reference rung. The remaining gap is source-table/profile and envelope semantics, not more decorative patch layers. The report now also emits source-table WAVs for listening. The hardened reader parses the upstream GPL instrument bank at commit c5c912131b31df5fdf372d2f06a25aaf2375837f: 1318/1318 .xiz files; optional tests write a ranked report, a handled/counted/unknown coverage map, and five upstream PAD listening fixtures under ignored artifacts. Coverage currently says PAD is the strongest exercised path, while ADD, SUB, active LFO routing, free envelopes, formant motion, effects, PAD formant/peak/shelf filters, and several PAD OscilGen filter/waveshaper subtypes remain workout buckets. Native AquaSynth filters now include `bpf` and `notch`; Zyn PAD analog and state-variable LP/HP/BPF/notch filters lower through existing cutoff/order/Q fields. `RebuildFirstPadAsAquaSynthScript` now generates first-pass per-preset PAD `.aqua` candidates from the first enabled PAD kit item, OSCIL harmonic magnitudes, oracle-reported table base frequency, basic volume, and simple rate/level envelope scaffolding while reporting missing bandwidth/profile/filter semantics. The worst target is `olivers-100/0032-Drum Kit.xiz`, with 16 enabled kit items, mixed ADD/SUB/PAD engines, 106 envelopes, 25 free envelopes, 73 LFOs, 37 filters, and 3 formant filters. The first rebuilds show AquaSynth can sketch small Zyn fixtures with existing voices/macros/formants/noise, and the upstream survey correctly pushed named kit/layer routing first. `layer` owns name, engine tag, optional key range metadata, default gain, and effect-send labels while lowering to ordinary voices. `harmonics` owns authored ratio/gain partial banks under those layers. `spectrum` owns PAD-style root/spread/partial clouds under named layers and generated table base frequency; playback `freq` remains note authority. Staged `env=rl` now applies to voices as well as operators, preserving Note as gate authority and envelope data as level-shape authority. AquaSynth still does not claim Zyn ADDsynth phase/bandwidth details, full Zyn PAD harmonic-profile/randomness/pitch-zone behavior, arbitrary free-envelope point curves, or richer formant morphing.
zyn_reference_path.next: Continue Zyn PAD parity from the sampled OscilGen stage-order, multi-kit PAD, Zyn-curve static global-filter, low-pass/high-pass order, PAD frequency-detune, low-pass/high-pass filter-envelope, logarithmic Zyn envelope-time, generated Zyn PAD output-calibration, low-pass note tracking, explicit low-pass Q, Q+order cascade, one-based OscilGen oscillator-filter index, base-function-7 selected-table-root, base-function phase modulation, and adaptive-harmonics paths. Ghost Ensemble now expands its power-base multi-harmonic OscilGen source, applies Zyn filter note tracking from `freq_track`, emits `lpf_q` from the Zyn analog filter `q`, and preserves low-pass slope by cascading resonant Faust stages according to `lpf_order`; latest Ghost log-mel is 0.369402, envelope distance is 0.363685, RMS ratio is 1.054875, centroid ratio is 1.006808, and score is 0.57229. DoublePadBass is accepted by ear and latest survey reports log-mel 0.247674, envelope distance 0.208351, RMS ratio 0.888011, score 0.650319. Soft Pad is accepted by ear. Organ Choir now maps kit0 HP2/stage/filter-envelope motion and kit1's selected table root, `base_function_modulation=1`, and `adaptive_harmonics=1`; latest Organ log-mel is 0.471483, envelope distance 0.302978, RMS ratio 0.878, centroid ratio 1.011328, and score 0.541933. Its kit1 PAD table energy now matches the wind-body shape much more closely: 400-800 Hz 0.2713 vs Zyn 0.2780 and 8-16 kHz 0.1471 vs Zyn 0.1355. ZynPadReference has test-only `harmonics` and `selected` modes; selected roots are diagnostic/source-specific, not a global default, because global selected roots regressed accepted DoublePadBass. sin2x remains loud/concentrated after the one-based filter-index fix, so do not claim full Zyn PAD parity until broader OscilGen filtering/waveshaping/adaptive-harmonic behavior and note/global filter LFO ownership are mapped or deliberately excluded.

note_and_envelope_model.rule: Notes own frequency, gate duration, and host/MIDI source. Envelopes own ADSR level shape.
note_and_envelope_model.sfxr_mapping: SFXR sustain is note gate duration. SFXR punch maps to lower ADSR sustain level plus voice gain compensation so the implicit attack peak stays at 1 and the held level sits below it.
note_and_envelope_model.faust_surface: One-shot patches use generated ADSR over patch age and note gate duration. MIDI mono/poly patches emit Faust's standard freq/gain/gate controls and [midi:on][nvoices:n] metadata so Faust architectures own note allocation.

runtime_parameters.rule: Patch parameters are runtime controls, not compile-time constants.
runtime_parameters.faust_surface: Faust UI controls expose parameter zones that hosts can update with setParamValue/getParamValue-style APIs.
runtime_parameters.aquasynth_requirement: AquaSynth DSL patches declare stable exposed parameters with path/default/min/max/step metadata so callers can vary sound without recompiling.
runtime_parameters.binding_rule: Parameter binding uses field-site references such as `lpf=@/macro/brightness` and `/opgraphs/0/operators/2/level`; the field owns meaning and the parameter owns the runtime value. Avoid target-routing semantics unless a new graph authority is deliberately mapped.

last_verified.command: dotnet build AquaSynth.slnx --no-restore; dotnet test tests\AquaSynth.Dsl.Tests\AquaSynth.Dsl.Tests.csproj --no-build -v minimal; dotnet pack src\AquaSynth.Dsl\AquaSynth.Dsl.csproj -c Release --no-restore
last_verified.result: Renamed local repo, namespaces, package metadata, docs, state, and remote wiring to AquaSynth/AquaSynth-rs; test assembly passed 108 and pack produced AquaSynth.Dsl.0.1.0-aquasynth.1.nupkg
